rationale: Re-shape attempt_01 by lifting next_ensures into a universally quantified forall (different trigger surface than the previous if-guarded decomposition) and pre-prove the per-run unfolding via a helper lemma so z3 can instantiate it at each run without re-deriving the open-spec body each time; the view-extensionality helpers are emitted so z3 can collapse Seq/struct-level view equality if it does derive component-wise equality.

helper_lemmas:
proof fn lemma_next_ensures_decompose(
    pre_self_: HostState,
    pre_netc: NetClient,
    post_self_: HostState,
    post_netc: NetClient,
    r: (bool, Ghost<EventResults>),
)
    requires
        HostState::next_ensures(pre_self_, pre_netc, post_self_, post_netc, r),
    ensures
        r.0 == post_netc.ok(),
        r.0 ==> post_self_.invariants(&post_netc.my_end_point()),
        r.0 ==> HostState::next(pre_self_@, post_self_@, r.1@.ios),
        r.0 ==> r.1@.event_seq() == r.1@.ios,
        (r.0 || r.1@.sends.len() > 0)
            ==> post_netc.history() == pre_netc.history() + r.1@.event_seq(),
        r.1@.well_typed_events(),
{
}

proof fn lemma_event_results_view_extensionality(a: EventResults, b: EventResults)
    requires
        a.recvs == b.recvs,
        a.clocks == b.clocks,
        a.sends == b.sends,
        a.ios == b.ios,
    ensures
        a.view() == b.view(),
{
    assert(a.view() =~= b.view());
}

proof fn lemma_net_client_view_extensionality(a: NetClient, b: NetClient)
    requires
        a.history() == b.history(),
        a.my_end_point() == b.my_end_point(),
    ensures
        a.view() == b.view(),
{
    assert(a.view() =~= b.view());
}
